resources.string: Configure name, avatar, model, and parameters
Manage quick messages, prompt injections, lorebooks and skills
Enable local tools like JavaScript engine
Configure MCP server tools for the assistant
Manage assistant memory and recent chat references
Set system prompts, message templates, and regex rules
Configure custom request headers and body parameters
No lorebooks available, create one in Prompts
No mode injections available, create one in Prompts
No quick messages available, create one in Extensions
No skills available, install one in Extensions
Set background image for assistant chat page
Set the default chat model for the assistant. If not set, the global default chat model will be used
Controls how many historical messages will be sent to the model as context. Messages exceeding this number will be ignored, only the most recent N messages will be retained, which can save tokens
Are you sure you want to delete this assistant? This action cannot be undone.
Use global shared memory instead of assistant-isolated memory. When enabled, all assistants share the same memory storage
Allow the AI to ask you questions for clarification
Read ringer mode, music-active state, headphone connection, and output devices.
Read battery level, charging state, plug type, temperature, and health.
Read and set screen brightness. Setting requires WRITE_SETTINGS.
Read or write plain text from the device clipboard
Download files from URLs into the public Downloads folder, and save text content (notes, transcripts, jokes, scripts) as files there.
Execute JavaScript code with QuickJS engine for calculations and scripting
Let the assistant schedule prompts to run in the background later — once or on an interval. Jobs survive app close and reboot.
Let the assistant run shell commands and transfer files on remote machines via SSH/SFTP. Saved hosts, password or private-key auth, accept-new host-key checking.

Run the assistant as a Telegram bot — send/receive messages, files, manage /commands. Configured entirely via tools (set token, whitelist, default chat, then enable). Foreground service handles long-poll.
Run the LLM as a Telegram bot — chat with it remotely from any device.
Trigger-based automations: when something happens on the device, run a sequence of tools.
Time-based jobs the assistant scheduled — one-shots and recurring cron expressions.
Diagnose and fix the app — permissions, services, database, network, Termux.
Tools you\'ve permanently granted via "Always Allow". Revoke any time.
Tools below run without asking, ever, on this device. Revoke if you no longer trust the model with this capability.
No tools have been "Always Allow"-granted yet. The bot will ask before running any side-effecting tool.
From @BotFather. The bot must be stopped to edit.
Where outbound messages go when the LLM omits chat_id (e.g. cron job alerts). Use your numeric Telegram user id.
Comma-separated. Empty = nobody. Find your id with @userinfobot.
When started, the bot also auto-starts on device boot.
Battery optimization: not whitelisted - tap to fix

When the screen turns off, aggressive OEM ROMs (Xiaomi, OPPO, OnePlus, Vivo) cut network for non-whitelisted apps. The bot will only deliver during occasional maintenance windows. Tap to whitelist RikkaHub.
Play and stop audio from a file path or URL.
Notify Android\'s media scanner about new files so they appear in gallery/music apps.
Post Android notifications on the user\'s behalf.
This tool needs system access. You\'ll be sent to the system settings page to grant it. Return here when done.
List device sensors and read short samples (accelerometer, gyroscope, light, etc.).
Open the system share sheet to send text or a URL to other apps.
Read total/free/used storage on internal and external volumes.
Read SIM and network info (carrier, network type). Requires READ_PHONE_STATE.
Get current date, weekday, time, and timezone info from the device
Show a brief toast popup over whatever is currently on screen.
Turn the camera flashlight on or off.
Let the AI speak text aloud to you using the device TTS engine
Vibrate the device with a duration or pattern.
Read and set audio stream volumes. Ring/notification streams require DND access.
Read current Wi-Fi connection (SSID, IP, signal). Requires location permission for SSID.
Maximum number of tokens to generate. Leave empty for no limit.

When memory is enabled, the model will try to actively record your information during conversations and use it in subsequent conversations. This feature requires the model to support tool calls to work properly
Controls the content format of chat messages before being sent to the LLM, usually no need to modify
Template does not contain {{ message }}, user messages will not be sent
When enabled, the assistant will reference recent chat history to provide more contextual responses
Define regex patterns to transform messages before sending/receiving
Visual Only (affects display only, not actual message)
Whether to enable streaming output for messages
Automatically inject time reminders before messages with large time gaps to help AI understand conversation intervals
Please don\'t modify this value unless you know what you\'re doing
Use assistant avatar and name instead of model avatar and name in chats
Export app data to a file
The app needs to restart for the changes to take effect.
⏰ Scheduled jobs run autonomously without per-tool approval. Approving this lets the job run with full tool access whenever it fires. Hardline-blocked commands still cannot run.
🚨 ON. Every tool runs without prompting. Hardline blocks (rm -rf /, mkfs, shutdown …) still apply.
Off. Side-effecting tools prompt for approval before running.

Every tool — local shell, SSH, file writes, screen automation, Telegram outbound, MCP — will run without asking you. The agent could send messages, run commands on remote hosts, take photos, write files, schedule jobs, all without confirmation.

HARDLINE protections still apply: rm -rf /, mkfs, shutdown / reboot, dd to block devices, fork bomb, encoded payload pipes — those keep blocking.

This is a foot-gun. Only enable if you trust the model + assistant config + your own prompts to behave responsibly.
Specify what to preserve or focus on
Compress the conversation into a summary to save tokens
Not enough messages to compress. Reduce the number of messages to keep or add more messages.
⚠️ Compressing context will reset all messages in the current conversation
Please chat first before editing the title
Export conversation as a markdown file
Current message count: %1$d. This may cause the application to crash or lag, or even lose this conversation data. Please create a new conversation as soon as possible to continue chatting.
This conversation data is too large
"The above content is generated by artificial intelligence and may contain inaccuracies. "
Manage skill packages for AI to load on demand
Are you sure you want to delete all conversations? This action cannot be undone.
Select aspect ratio for generated images
Are you sure you want to cancel image generation?
Number of images to generate each time
Select AI model for image generation
Describe the image you want to generate...
No available AI providers, please add in settings
No text content available to copy
Camera permission is required to take photos
Some permissions have been permanently denied, please go to the app settings to manually enable them:
In order to provide a better user experience, the app requires the following permissions:
Required on Android 17+ for SSH and the LAN web server
Click the button below to add
Are you sure you want to delete "%s"? This action cannot be undone.
Tap the + button at the bottom right to add a quick message
Let the model automatically determine reasoning level.
The model will use a large amount of reasoning to answer questions.
The model will use a small amount of reasoning to answer questions.
The model will use more reasoning to answer questions.
Turn off reasoning functionality, the model will directly answer questions.
Are you sure you want to regenerate this message?

Official free AI service, using user sponsorship funds to provide some free models for new users as fallback, no API key required. Has strict rate limits, if you have purchased your own provider, please use your own provider as much as possible.
The app crashed on the last launch. You can switch to a different assistant to resolve the issue.
Enter keywords and press Search to find messages
This will clear the existing index and re-scan all chat history. The more messages, the longer it takes. Continue?
Use pure black background in dark theme, suitable for AMOLED screens
Automatically collapse thinking content after thinking is complete
Automatically play TTS after assistant message generation completes
Whether to display model icons in chat list messages
Automatically collapse code blocks by default
Automatically wrap long lines in code blocks
Create a new conversation when the app starts
Automatically scroll to the bottom when AI is generating content
Enable blur effect on chat input bar
Render mathematical expressions and formulas using LaTeX syntax
Enable haptic feedback when messages are being generated

At four in the morning, I saw the begonia flowers still awake. I am often like this — lying awake alone in the quiet night, thinking: if someone is thinking of me at this moment, that would be wonderful.
Show real-time notification updates during message generation (Android 15+)
Move Message Jumper to left side
Show a notification when a message is generated if the app is not in the foreground
When pasting text exceeds the threshold, automatically save it as a file attachment
Press Enter to send message instead of adding a new line
Whether to show bubble background for assistant messages
Whether to show message date below nickname or model name
Display line numbers in code blocks
Display quick jump buttons on the right when scrolling the chat list
Whether to display model names in chat messages
Whether to expand and show the thinking process by default during generation
Display token usage and message count
Whether to display app update notifications
Whether to display user avatar in chat messages
Skip crop interface after importing images or taking photos
When enabled, TTS will only read content within quotation marks
Use the animated app icon instead of the default circular loading indicator
Scroll pages with volume keys, ideal for e-ink screens
Add custom HTTP headers for MCP server requests
Whether to enable this MCP server
Paste MCP Server JSON config, supports standard mcpServers format
No valid MCP Server configuration found
Display name for the MCP server
URL address for SSE transport server
URL address for Streamable HTTP server
Cannot view tool list when adding MCP Server, please check back after adding
Select the transport protocol type for the MCP server
Model used for optical character recognition on images
Model used to generate conversation suggestions, recommend using fast and inexpensive models
Model used to summarize conversation titles, recommend using fast and inexpensive models
The model used for translation features
Select built-in tools supported by the model. These tools are directly provided by the API without requiring app support. Currently, this feature is only supported by the official Gemini API.
You haven\'t configured API and model yet, please configure first
Set default models for each feature
View app usage instructions and help documentation
If you like it, sponsor the author\'s work
Manage prompt injections, skills and more
RikkaHub - Open Source Android AI Assistant\n\nWebsite: https://rikka-ai.com/
You have launched the app over 100 times! If you enjoy using it, please consider supporting the developer.

LAN Address: Uses device WiFi IP, accessible to other devices on the same network. Unavailable on mobile data.\nLocal Address: Uses localhost, accessible on this device only.\nmDNS Address: Uses .local hostname, accessible by name from other devices on the same network without knowing the IP.
Allow you access RikkaHub via Web
Require password verification when accessing the web interface
Listen only on localhost (127.0.0.1), disable LAN and mDNS access
Password required for web interface access
Click the button below to add models
Are you sure you want to delete this provider? This action cannot be undone.
Failed to parse QR code from image: %s
Configure custom provider settings for this specific model. Will inherit common properties from parent provider.
Response API is OpenAI\'s next-generation API. Most providers do not support it. If you don\'t know what this is, don\'t enable it.
Custom name for this TTS provider
Control the pitch of speech (0.1 - 2.0, 1.0 is normal)
Control the speed of speech (0.1 - 3.0, 1.0 is normal)
Hello, this is a text-to-speech test.
Voice to use for speech synthesis
**Leading global AI capability provider, accelerating AGI to benefit humanity.**
Are you sure you want to delete file "%s"?
No SKILL.md found inside the zip.
Zip contains an unsafe path; refusing to extract.
Unsupported file. Use a .md file or a .zip with SKILL.md inside.
Zip is too large (cap 20 MB / 200 files).
File is too large (cap 1 MB for .md files).
The file is empty — nothing to import.
Type a prompt to test this skill…
(No text response — see image parts above.)
Timed out after 2 minutes. The skill is still running in the background.
Are you sure you want to delete "%s"? This action cannot be undone.
Tap the + button at the bottom right to add a Skill
Enter a GitHub repository URL, and the SKILL.md file in the root directory will be downloaded automatically.
Cannot find the name field, please check the frontmatter format
Paste the full SKILL.md file content
Save failed, please check the Skill format
Tag with this name already exists
The current model does not have tool calling enabled. Please enable the model\'s tool capabilities in the settings to use features like search/MCP.
Translation result will be shown here
Downloading, please check status bar for progress
Not all models support depth adjustment. Refer to the model and provider documentation.
Read the device\'s current location (latitude, longitude, accuracy).
Search or list device contacts (name, phone numbers, emails).
Read recent calls (incoming, outgoing, missed) with duration and timestamps.
Read or search SMS inbox messages.
Open the system camera so the user can take a photo and return its file path.
Record audio from the microphone for a bounded duration to a local file.
Listen to the microphone and convert spoken words to text.
Show the system biometric prompt to verify the user\'s identity.
Lets RikkaHub tap, swipe, scroll, read screen content, and take screenshots when the LLM uses the Screen automation tools.
Let the LLM tap, swipe, scroll, read on-screen content, and take screenshots via Android\'s accessibility service.
Open installed apps by package name (e.g. Termux, Settings) and list available apps. Pairs with screen automation for full UI control.
Let the LLM read incoming notifications from other apps. Whitelisted apps also auto-forward to your default Telegram chat. Configure in Settings -> Notifications.
Termux not installed - install from github.com/termux/termux-app/releases
Toggle on to grant the RUN_COMMAND permission
Almost ready. Open Termux and run: mkdir -p ~/.termux && echo \'allow-external-apps=true\' >> ~/.termux/termux.properties — then force-stop Termux and reopen. Tap to verify.
Connected - commands run with captured output
Termux did not respond. Open Termux and run: mkdir -p ~/.termux && echo \'allow-external-apps=true\' >> ~/.termux/termux.properties — then force-stop Termux and reopen it.

RUN_COMMAND permission is granted. Termux still needs `allow-external-apps=true` in `~/.termux/termux.properties` before the assistant can run commands.

Open Termux and paste the command below, then force-stop Termux from app info and reopen it.

The row will turn green after you tap Verify.
Command copied — paste it into Termux.

The toggle is on, but no bot token is set yet.

Easy way: ask the assistant in chat — "Set up the Telegram bot, my token is …, my user id is …, set me as default chat, enable it."

Manual way: message @BotFather on Telegram with /newbot for a token, and @userinfobot with /start for your numeric user id. Then ask the assistant or paste them in Settings → Telegram bot.
Heads up: scheduled jobs use the tools you have enabled. If a job calls SSH, Telegram, or any other tool, those toggles must also be on.
RUN_COMMAND permission not granted. Toggle Termux off then on to retry the system prompt.
Termux replied but with unexpected output: %1$s
Read, write, list, search, copy, move, delete files.
Let the assistant add, edit, and remove MCP servers. The assistant can install an MCP in Termux or on a remote SSH host, then wire it into the app — without you opening Settings.

Let the assistant manage the External Automation Intent API — the path that lets Tasker / MacroDroid / Locale / ADB fire RUN_TASK intents at the assistant. Defaults to OFF; the assistant can flip it on, add trusted caller packages, and audit the recent invocation log.

Let the assistant check GitHub for newer releases and build a redacted bug-report ZIP on demand. The bug report includes the last few thousand logcat lines (with secrets redacted) and the app version + device info — and explicitly excludes your conversations, tokens, and saved hosts.

Let the assistant dispatch focused sub-tasks (research, multi-step work) as independent LLM runs in the background. Each run is approval-gated, capped (default 3 concurrent per assistant, 16 global), and HARDLINE still applies inside.
Let the assistant inspect its running token totals against the per-assistant soft / hard caps and self-throttle. Read-only — auto-stop integration with the streaming pipeline ships in a follow-up.
Status, recent actions, and screenshot diagnostics for the screen-automation service.
RikkaHub needs accessibility permission to perform taps, swipes, and read the screen for the LLM. Open Settings → Accessibility → Installed Apps → RikkaHub and turn it on.
Display over other apps: Not granted
Shows a small \"The agent is working\" pill at the top of the screen while the agent is automating, so you always know when it is driving the UI. Optional but recommended.
Auto-discovered list of every permission RikkaHub uses, with a one-tap grant button for each.
This will permanently remove %1$s and all its configured models. You can re-add it later.

On-device. Requires Pixel 8/9/10 with the AICore app installed AND enrollment in the GenAI Prompt-API early-access program (separate opt-in). If you see ErrorCode 606 / FEATURE_NOT_FOUND on first run, you have AICore installed but not the Prompt-API channel — request access via the GenAI Prompt-API early-access form, then reboot. Tool calling is supported via prompt engineering — Gemini Nano emits <tool_call> markup which RikkaHub parses and dispatches.
Notification access status, recent activity log, and the per-app whitelist that auto-forwards to Telegram.
RikkaHub needs notification access to read incoming notifications. Open Settings → Notification access → Notification access and enable RikkaHub.
Default Telegram chat: not set (auto-route disabled)
Apps whose notifications auto-forward to your default Telegram chat. Empty = nothing forwarded. Toggle each app on or off in the picker.
No installed apps match your filter.

Skip the LLM for high-confidence requests like \"what\'s my battery\", \"what time is it\", or \"list workflows\". The matching tool runs directly and you get an instant answer. Conservative — anything ambiguous still goes to the model. Logged to logcat (tag: FastPathRouter).

Let the assistant install skills from a URL OR from text it already has (e.g. fetched via SSH / curl / MCP for authenticated servers). Native, openclaw, and Hermes formats. Per-call approval required for every install (Always Allow is disabled — you review the source each time).

Let the assistant run JavaScript skills (calculate hash, query Wikipedia, generate QR codes, render maps, etc.) inside a hidden WebView. The skill\'s code defines what it does; per-call approval each time. Compatible with skills written for Google AI Edge Gallery — drop them into your skills folder and they just work.

Let the assistant pre-fill system apps for you to finalise: Calendar event, new Contact, email composer, SMS composer, WiFi settings, Maps. Nothing is saved or sent without your tap in the destination app.

Workflows are enabled. Some trigger types need permissions the toggle can't request:

• Geofences (you arrive somewhere): Settings → Apps → RikkaHub → Permissions → Location → Allow all the time.
• Notification arrives: Settings → Apps → Special access → Notification access → enable RikkaHub.
• App launches/closes: Settings → Accessibility → enable RikkaHub.
• Bluetooth connect/disconnect: grant BLUETOOTH_CONNECT when the system asks.

Schedule, WiFi, headphones, power, screen on/off, and battery triggers work right out of the box. The assistant will flag any extra setup at the moment you author a workflow that needs it.
Let the assistant create automations that run on triggers — WiFi connect, schedule, geofence, notification arrival, and more.
No workflows yet. Ask your assistant to create one.

A workflow is a trigger plus a list of actions. The trigger is what starts it (WiFi connects, a schedule fires, you arrive at a place, a notification arrives). The actions are tool calls run in order — the same tools you can run in chat.

Conditions filter when a triggered workflow actually fires ("only after sunset", "only if charging"). All conditions must pass.

Workflows run in headless mode — your assistant pre-authorised the tools when it created the workflow, so each fire doesn't prompt you. The HARDLINE filter still blocks dangerous shell commands and writes to system paths regardless. You'll see the result in the workflow's history list.
This removes the workflow and its history. Triggers it owned (geofences, schedules, broadcasts) are torn down on the next reconciliation. Cannot be undone.
No scheduled jobs yet. Ask your assistant to create one.

Scheduled jobs are time-based. Two timing types: "once" (fires at a single absolute moment) and "cron" (fires on a recurring expression like every weekday at 9 a.m.).

Two run modes: "direct" runs a fixed list of tool calls with no LLM (free, deterministic). "LLM" sends a prompt to the assistant at fire time and lets the model decide what to do.

Fires happen even when the app is closed — they're scheduled with WorkManager and survive reboot. The HARDLINE filter still blocks dangerous shell commands. Each fire is recorded in the job's history.
This removes the job, its run history, and any pending fire. Cannot be undone.
Job is paused — enable it first.
AI-driven web browser and skill viewer.
AI-driven web browser. 17 individually-togglable tools — configure read/write access in Settings → Browser.
Lightweight HTTP GET/POST tool. 30s timeout, response body capped at 8 KB. Requires approval on each call.
Send SMS messages programmatically. Requires SEND_SMS permission. Approval required per call.
Set the home or lock screen wallpaper from a local image. Approval required per call.
Generate, sign, encrypt, and decrypt with hardware-backed keys (Android Keystore). 7 tools.
Read and write NDEF records on NFC tags. Requires the app to be in foreground for tag detection.
Lets file tools work on USB drives, SD cards, Downloads, and cloud storage. Each tree requires a one-time grant via the SAF picker.
Create and extract .zip archives directly. No password support in v1. Works on file:// and content:// URIs.
Drive the active text field through the co-signed agent-keyboard app: type, read, press keys, delete, move the cursor, and select text. 8 tools. Write tools require approval per call.

Keyboard control needs the companion agent-keyboard app installed AND set as the active keyboard. Install it, then go to Settings > System > Languages & input > On-screen keyboard and switch to agent-keyboard. Without both, every keyboard tool will return an error.
agent-keyboard installed - set it as the active keyboard to use these tools
Launch the browser to about:blank — useful to log in once before letting the AI take over.
Wipe cookies, local storage, and cached pages. Per-tool toggles are kept.
This clears all cookies, local storage, and cached pages. You will need to re-log in to any site the AI was using.
Read the page\'s URL and title.
Extract readable text from the page.
Extract simplified HTML from the page.
List all link URLs and labels.
Pause until a CSS selector appears.
Execute arbitrary JS in the page (HARDLINE-checked).
Let the AI signal it has finished a task.
DuckDuckGo (default) — switching takes effect once browser_search ships.
Hard time budget for each browser tool call. Range 10 s to 10 min.
Hard cap on one AI-driven browser task to bound runaway loops. Range 1 min to 60 min.
Tell the AI what to do…
Run open models on this device. ~1.5 GB download required (Qwen2.5 1.5B Q8).
RikkaHub needs to download a starter model for this provider. The download is ~1.5 GB (Qwen2.5 1.5B Q8). Continue?
Off by default. Some devices crash during inference on the GPU/NPU backend with this runtime — leave off if you hit a crash. CPU-only is slower but reliable.

Override the curated default. The model file\'s KV cache is the hard ceiling — Qwen ekv4096 caps at 4096 regardless. Capable models (Gemma 4 E2B = 32000) benefit from a larger value. Leave blank to use the curated default.
Recovered: the local model crashed last session on %1$s — switched to CPU automatically. Tap to dismiss; toggle “Try GPU acceleration” below to retry.
Need about %1$d MB of free RAM (model is %2$d MB plus runtime headroom) but only %3$d MB is available. Close other apps or pick a smaller model.
Or paste a HuggingFace URL (/blob/ or /resolve/ both work)
Tool calling: prompt-engineered. Accuracy varies per model.
%1$d model(s) installed. Manage them in the Models tab.
Remove \"%1$s\" from your device? This deletes the file (you\'ll need to download it again to use it).
Invalid URL: must be https and well-formed
Curated by Google AI Edge Gallery. Pick one for one-tap install.
Usually 1. Not recommended to change for reasoning models.
Microphone permission is required for speech recognition
Custom name for this ASR provider
OpenAI API key used by the realtime websocket
Optional ISO language code, such as zh or en
Optional hint for names, terms, or formatting
Audio kept before detected speech, in milliseconds
Silence duration that ends a turn, in milliseconds
Server VAD sensitivity, set 0 to disable VAD
Optional language code, e.g. zh-CN, en-US, ja-JP
Connection lost — maximum reconnect attempts reached
Changing the skill name is not allowed (the name field must be \"%1$s\")
Please stop generation before deleting messages
